response:
**Summary:**

The internet originated from US Department of Defense research in the 1960s, with ARPANET demonstrating the first network message in 1969 and TCP/IP protocols establishing the modern foundation in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee's invention of the World Wide Web in 1989, followed by the Mosaic browser in 1993, democratized access and fueled the dot-com boom of the late 1990s, though many companies failed in the 2001 crash. The mid-2000s saw social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), smartphones (including the transformative 2007 iPhone), and cloud computing (like AWS in 2006) make the internet ubiquitous. Today, over 5 billion people are connected, making the internet essential to virtually every aspect of modern life.
